notes: Only pre-fix failure: calibration-responses.md was written under agent-company/ (stale v2 path), violating the v2.1 contract. SKILL.md patched to write under .claude/agent-calibration/ instead; eval-2 and eval-3 ran post-fix and passed 20/20.
Produced a domain-sound 5-agent company with UK alcohol regulatory awareness; ad-hoc schema — no v2.1 structural conformity.
Four agents (Outreach, Social, Finance, Producer-Ops), domain-specific frameworks, 24/24 calibration. Calibration correctly placed under agent-calibration/ per the v2.1 patch.
Competent four-agent business-ops team but generic schema. Only two assertions passed (no agent-company/ folder; non-technical scope respected).

Six agents including Supabase/local-SEO specialists justified by the brief. References real codebase paths: src/app/(dashboard)/, src/app/(marketing)/, tailwind.config.ts, supabase/migrations/. 36/36 simulated calibration passes.
First run of this eval failed on sandbox permissions (executor couldn't Write to the sandbox dir). Retry succeeded with identical prompt and verified directory.
Six agents with sensible adjacent specialists (local-SEO, Supabase architect) but no v2.1 structural conformity. Two assertions passed.
Post-fix runs (eval-2, eval-3) hit 20/20. Pre-fix eval-1 hit 19/20 on the agent-company/ calibration path contradiction. The fix stuck.

Without-skill is roughly 2/10 on average — it produces a useful team (good domain coverage) but cannot hit the v2.1 structural contract (6-section body, memory: project, COMPANY.md column schema, v2.1 Standing Instructions). Every discriminating assertion discriminates.
Skill trade-off: ~2.7× wall time and ~1.6× tokens, buys +83.3 pp of pass rate.

Grader feedback across runs suggested the same improvements: (a) quality-gate assertion should name an output artefact, not rely on transcript inspection; (b) `no agent-company/ folder` is a trivial gate — keep but don't count as discriminating; (c) add a cross-agent routing-coherence assertion (would two agents' `description` fields auto-route disjointly?); (d) persist executor transcripts to outputs_dir so graders don't need substitutes.
Sandbox reliability: 1 of 6 runs hit a Write-denial on first attempt (eval-3 with-skill). Retry with identical prompt succeeded — intermittent, not prompt-caused.
